program.name: Tiny Leela browser engine dovetail research
program.objective: Build a tiny in-browser Leela-like chess engine with policy/WDL neural evaluation, PUCT search, ONNX Runtime Web deployment, and verified chess correctness.

L000.title: baseline trajectory
L000.hypothesis: Improve from the root baseline while scheduler spawns diverse lanes.

L001.title: True research lane: PUCT search policy experiments
L001.hypothesis: Compare structural root/search policy choices against fixed evaluator fixtures under benchmark tiny_leela_phase_b_fixed_metrics_v1.

L002.title: True research lane: PUCT search policy experiments
L002.hypothesis: Once the substrate is stable, compare structural PUCT choices such as FPU, policy temperature, root noise, and uncertainty-based playout allocation against fixed evaluator fixtures.

L003.title: True research lane: browser inference backends
L003.hypothesis: Compare ORT Web WASM/SIMD, WebGPU/FP16, worker batching, and model caching for latency and compatibility without changing chess semantics.

L004.title: True research lane: tiny student architectures
L004.hypothesis: Compare micro and small conv student architecture specs under fixed Phase B policy/WDL metrics to find better strength-per-byte candidates.

N0001.hypothesis: Create minimal TypeScript/Node test scaffold so engine infrastructure can be built and verified in future lanes.

decision.reason: Bootstrap score rose from 0 to 32 and checks pass. The scheduler is currently treating foundational infrastructure as normal ideas, which is useful for tracking but can feel over-fragmented before the engine skeleton exists.

N0002.hypothesis: Convert the over-fragmented pre-work into an explicit L000 foundation substrate and separate true research-lane ideas from shared engine infrastructure.

decision.reason: The setup now has a minimum shared engine substrate and the idea queue distinguishes blocking pre-work from differentiated lanes. Bootstrap score improved from 32 to 98 with checks passing.

N0003.hypothesis: Clean benchmark/check scripts so Node's built-in test runner does not receive an invalid Vitest-style --run argument.

decision.reason: Metric remains 98, but validation output is cleaner and checks still pass. This is a stabilize-only cleanup, not a research lane.

N0004.hypothesis: Finish taxonomy cleanup by moving remaining spec/schema/gauntlet scaffold tasks out of the queued research-lane pool.

decision.reason: The idea queue now has 9 foundation milestones separated from 8 true research lanes. Checks pass with score 98.

N0005.hypothesis: Complete Phase A/B separation by documenting lab/product substrate vs frozen evaluation metrics and making Phase B metric readiness executable.

decision.reason: Phase A/B are now explicit: build substrate and evaluation infrastructure are pre-work, while Phase C research optimizes fixed metrics like policy_top1_acc, wdl_cross_entropy, fixed_playout_suite_score, latency, and model size. Readiness score improved to 130 and checks pass.

N0006.hypothesis: For the first search lane experiment, compare a structural root visit policy that preserves evaluator prior mass against the previous argmax-only root choice on frozen Phase B fixtures.

decision.reason: On the fixed 3-position fixture suite, argmax scored 33.849477 with CE 13.078683, while prior_proportional scored 73.468924 with CE 1.101706 and top1/top3 remained 1.0. This is an early toy-fixture result, not a chess-strength claim.

N0007.hypothesis: Compare a progressive WebGPU/WASM fallback runtime strategy against a WebGPU-only baseline under fixed browser capability profiles without changing chess semantics.

decision.reason: Progressive fallback scored 83.525 with 3/4 compatible profiles and 147.5ms median estimated latency; manual WebGPU-only baseline scored 44.575 with 2/4 compatible profiles and 542.5ms median. This is a fixed-profile runtime strategy result, not measured ORT latency yet.

N0008.hypothesis: A sqrt-prior root distribution may smooth overconfident teacher priors while preserving top move, potentially improving suite score versus prior-proportional.

decision.reason: sqrt_prior scored 71.610243 with CE 1.167327, worse than prior_proportional's 73.468924 and CE 1.101706 while preserving top1/top3. On current fixed fixtures, preserving full teacher prior mass is better than square-root smoothing.

N0009.hypothesis: Rerun prior_proportional in the search lane after sqrt_prior ablation to confirm the current local best under fixed fixtures before moving to a different structural idea.

decision.reason: Result exactly reproduced fixed_playout_suite_score=73.468924 and CE=1.101706. Current deterministic suite has no noise, so further simple prior transforms are unlikely to beat exact teacher-prior preservation.

N0010.hypothesis: Formally log the argmax root policy as the search-lane baseline to make the prior_proportional improvement auditable in the research tree.

decision.reason: Argmax preserves top1/top3 on the tiny suite but collapses the teacher distribution, scoring only 33.849477 with CE 13.078683 versus prior_proportional 73.468924/CE 1.101706. This confirms distribution-sensitive metrics are necessary; top-k alone cannot distinguish these policies.

N0011.hypothesis: Follow scheduler assignment in L002 by rerunning the only non-degenerate current-best root policy before forcing rotation away from exhausted policy-only search transforms.

decision.reason: Deterministic result again equals fixed_playout_suite_score=73.468924 and CE=1.101706. This confirms the policy-only search lane has saturated: because the benchmark rewards matching teacher distribution, exact prior preservation is the optimum among current transforms.

N0012.hypothesis: Under scheduler pressure to continue L002, re-evaluate sqrt_prior as a second lane-local check to confirm it is not competitive with exact prior preservation.

decision.reason: sqrt_prior again scores 71.610243 vs prior_proportional 73.468924. This duplicate result reinforces that L002 should be paused under current fixtures; more policy-only transform work is tunnel vision.

N0013.hypothesis: A simpler WebGPU-with-WASM-fallback runtime strategy might match progressive fallback compatibility while avoiding progressive/caching complexity.

decision.reason: wasm_fallback scored 73.525 with same 3/4 compatible profiles and 147.5ms median as progressive_fallback, but loses cache/progressive loading bonus. Progressive_fallback remains the current L003 best at 83.525.

N0014.hypothesis: Formally log WebGPU-only runtime strategy as the L003 baseline to make fallback benefits auditable.

decision.reason: WebGPU-only scores 44.575 with 2/4 compatible profiles and 542.5ms median penalty due unsupported browsers. This confirms browser runtime research must optimize compatibility and fallback, not only fastest backend.

R0001.hypothesis: Create minimal TypeScript/Node test scaffold so engine infrastructure can be built and verified in future lanes.
R0001.implementation: Added package.json, tsconfig.json, src/index.ts, tests/smoke.test.mjs, and made checks only run tsc when TypeScript is installed locally.
R0001.learned: Bootstrap score rose from 0 to 32 and checks pass. The scheduler is currently treating foundational infrastructure as normal ideas, which is useful for tracking but can feel over-fragmented before the engine skeleton exists.
R0001.next_action: Treat core infra as L000 foundation/backlog or a milestone checklist, then spawn separate lanes only after board/movegen/codec/search interfaces exist.

R0002.hypothesis: Convert the over-fragmented pre-work into an explicit L000 foundation substrate and separate true research-lane ideas from shared engine infrastructure.
R0002.implementation: Added board/FEN parsing, UCI/action move codec, pseudo-legal move generation and makeMove, feature encoder, evaluator interface, minimal search entry point, move encoding/browser docs, substrate tests, and reclassified loaded infra ideas as foundation_milestone while appending true research lanes.
R0002.learned: The setup now has a minimum shared engine substrate and the idea queue distinguishes blocking pre-work from differentiated lanes. Bootstrap score improved from 32 to 98 with checks passing.
R0002.next_action: Continue L000 only for hard correctness gaps such as full legal check/perft fixtures; otherwise schedule true lanes for search/runtime/model/training/evaluation comparisons.

R0003.hypothesis: Clean benchmark/check scripts so Node's built-in test runner does not receive an invalid Vitest-style --run argument.
R0003.implementation: Removed npm test -- --run fallback from autoresearch.sh and autoresearch.checks.sh; both now call npm test directly.
R0003.learned: Metric remains 98, but validation output is cleaner and checks still pass. This is a stabilize-only cleanup, not a research lane.
R0003.next_action: Commit cleanup and mark the goal complete after confirming clean git status.

R0004.hypothesis: Finish taxonomy cleanup by moving remaining spec/schema/gauntlet scaffold tasks out of the queued research-lane pool.
R0004.implementation: Reclassified I0009, I0011, and I0012 as foundation_milestone with low priority; queued high-priority ideas are now true differentiated lanes only.
R0004.learned: The idea queue now has 9 foundation milestones separated from 8 true research lanes. Checks pass with score 98.
R0004.next_action: Use schedule_next for actual lane work; if scheduler still picks L000, interpret it as foundation hardening only.

R0005.hypothesis: Complete Phase A/B separation by documenting lab/product substrate vs frozen evaluation metrics and making Phase B metric readiness executable.
R0005.implementation: Added docs/research_phases.md, eval/benchmark_spec.json, eval/phase_b_metrics.mjs, tests/phase_b.test.mjs, npm eval:phase-b script, and checks that validate Phase B metrics. Updated autoresearch.md and readiness scoring to state tiny_leela_score is lab-readiness only.
R0005.learned: Phase A/B are now explicit: build substrate and evaluation infrastructure are pre-work, while Phase C research optimizes fixed metrics like policy_top1_acc, wdl_cross_entropy, fixed_playout_suite_score, latency, and model size. Readiness score improved to 130 and checks pass.
R0005.next_action: Commit Phase A/B freeze, then optionally start a new Phase C benchmark segment when a real lane has an executable candidate metric.

R0006.hypothesis: For the first search lane experiment, compare a structural root visit policy that preserves evaluator prior mass against the previous argmax-only root choice on frozen Phase B fixtures.
R0006.implementation: Added eval/search_suite.mjs with fixed metrics for root distributions, src/search/rootPolicy.ts with argmax/prior_proportional/sqrt_prior variants, an npm eval:search script, and a regression test proving prior_proportional improves distribution score over argmax while preserving top-1.
R0006.learned: On the fixed 3-position fixture suite, argmax scored 33.849477 with CE 13.078683, while prior_proportional scored 73.468924 with CE 1.101706 and top1/top3 remained 1.0. This is an early toy-fixture result, not a chess-strength claim.
R0006.next_action: Ablate sqrt_prior or add value-weighted one-ply root policy, but do not change the fixed benchmark fixtures in this lane.

R0007.hypothesis: Compare a progressive WebGPU/WASM fallback runtime strategy against a WebGPU-only baseline under fixed browser capability profiles without changing chess semantics.
R0007.implementation: Added src/browser/backendPlan.ts with webgpu_only, wasm_fallback, and progressive_fallback strategies; added eval/runtime_suite.mjs and tests/runtime_suite.test.mjs to score compatibility, median estimated latency, caching, and progressive loading under fixed profiles.
R0007.learned: Progressive fallback scored 83.525 with 3/4 compatible profiles and 147.5ms median estimated latency; manual WebGPU-only baseline scored 44.575 with 2/4 compatible profiles and 542.5ms median. This is a fixed-profile runtime strategy result, not measured ORT latency yet.
R0007.next_action: Continue runtime lane by replacing estimated latencies with measured mock/evaluator latency or add explicit model-artifact prerequisite before ORT backend experiments.

R0008.hypothesis: A sqrt-prior root distribution may smooth overconfident teacher priors while preserving top move, potentially improving suite score versus prior-proportional.
R0008.implementation: No code change; evaluated existing sqrt_prior variant in eval/search_suite.mjs using TINY_LEELA_SEARCH_VARIANT=sqrt_prior.
R0008.learned: sqrt_prior scored 71.610243 with CE 1.167327, worse than prior_proportional's 73.468924 and CE 1.101706 while preserving top1/top3. On current fixed fixtures, preserving full teacher prior mass is better than square-root smoothing.
R0008.next_action: Do not pursue sqrt_prior on this fixture suite. Next search-lane candidate should add value-aware one-ply selection or uncertainty-based allocation rather than another monotonic prior transform.

R0009.hypothesis: Rerun prior_proportional in the search lane after sqrt_prior ablation to confirm the current local best under fixed fixtures before moving to a different structural idea.
R0009.implementation: No code change; evaluated existing prior_proportional variant in eval/search_suite.mjs.
R0009.learned: Result exactly reproduced fixed_playout_suite_score=73.468924 and CE=1.101706. Current deterministic suite has no noise, so further simple prior transforms are unlikely to beat exact teacher-prior preservation.
R0009.next_action: Search lane should either add new per-move value fixtures to a new benchmark version or pause until model/evaluator outputs create non-teacher priors; scheduler should rotate to architecture/training/evaluation lanes.

R0010.hypothesis: Formally log the argmax root policy as the search-lane baseline to make the prior_proportional improvement auditable in the research tree.
R0010.implementation: No code change; evaluated existing argmax variant in eval/search_suite.mjs.
R0010.learned: Argmax preserves top1/top3 on the tiny suite but collapses the teacher distribution, scoring only 33.849477 with CE 13.078683 versus prior_proportional 73.468924/CE 1.101706. This confirms distribution-sensitive metrics are necessary; top-k alone cannot distinguish these policies.
R0010.next_action: Search lane has exhausted current fixed-policy-only variants; rotate away or create a new benchmark version with per-move value/search targets before further search work.

R0011.hypothesis: Follow scheduler assignment in L002 by rerunning the only non-degenerate current-best root policy before forcing rotation away from exhausted policy-only search transforms.
R0011.implementation: No code change; evaluated prior_proportional search suite under frozen fixtures.
R0011.learned: Deterministic result again equals fixed_playout_suite_score=73.468924 and CE=1.101706. This confirms the policy-only search lane has saturated: because the benchmark rewards matching teacher distribution, exact prior preservation is the optimum among current transforms.
R0011.next_action: Scheduler should rotate to a non-search lane or the program should add a new benchmark version with per-move values before additional search experiments.

R0012.hypothesis: Under scheduler pressure to continue L002, re-evaluate sqrt_prior as a second lane-local check to confirm it is not competitive with exact prior preservation.
R0012.implementation: No code change; reran existing sqrt_prior variant.
R0012.learned: sqrt_prior again scores 71.610243 vs prior_proportional 73.468924. This duplicate result reinforces that L002 should be paused under current fixtures; more policy-only transform work is tunnel vision.
R0012.next_action: Force rotation by creating/scheduling architecture or training lane; do not spend further L002 budget until benchmark evolves.

R0013.hypothesis: A simpler WebGPU-with-WASM-fallback runtime strategy might match progressive fallback compatibility while avoiding progressive/caching complexity.
R0013.implementation: No code change; evaluated existing wasm_fallback runtime strategy under fixed browser profiles.
R0013.learned: wasm_fallback scored 73.525 with same 3/4 compatible profiles and 147.5ms median as progressive_fallback, but loses cache/progressive loading bonus. Progressive_fallback remains the current L003 best at 83.525.
R0013.next_action: Do not pursue plain wasm_fallback unless complexity budget becomes a primary metric; runtime lane should next require measured latency or model artifact.

R0014.hypothesis: Formally log WebGPU-only runtime strategy as the L003 baseline to make fallback benefits auditable.
R0014.implementation: No code change; evaluated existing webgpu_only strategy under fixed browser profiles.
R0014.learned: WebGPU-only scores 44.575 with 2/4 compatible profiles and 542.5ms median penalty due unsupported browsers. This confirms browser runtime research must optimize compatibility and fallback, not only fastest backend.
R0014.next_action: Pause L003 until measured model/evaluator latency exists; rotate to architecture/training lane.
